name: Curie Charts
description: On-brand chart specimens for the Curie design system. Pure HTML/CSS/inline-SVG (no chart library); a chart's series colors map --chart-1/--chart-2 to Curie brand tokens. Rendered in the docs at /charts/.

charts.name: Bar Chart
charts.purpose: A small vertical bar chart built from divs (per-bar height set inline as a %) with a baseline axis, value labels above each bar, and a category label below. Magnitude is encoded in one hue; a single column can be emphasized with the active modifier.
charts.whenToUse: Use to compare a magnitude across a handful of named categories — briefs read per day, submissions per therapeutic area. Use bar-chart__bar--active to call out the peak or selected column.
charts.whenNotToUse: Do not use for change-over-time you want read as a continuous trend (use line-chart), for a single value (use stat-spark), or for more than ~8 categories where labels crowd.
anatomy: bar-chart__track (per-column full-height cell with the axis line)
bar-chart__bar (the column; height % inline, bar-chart__bar--active to emphasize)
charts.interactions: Static presentation specimen — values shown are illustrative and would be bound in-app. Bars brighten on :hover (pin with .is-hover for docs); no JS.

charts.name: Stacked Bar Chart
charts.purpose: A vertical bar chart that stacks two series per category with a legend, footer trend line, and caption — for comparing a two-part total across a time range (e.g. desktop vs mobile visitors per month). Rebuilt on-brand from the shadcn/recharts “stacked + legend” pattern using pure HTML/CSS — no chart library.
charts.whenToUse: When each category's value splits into two meaningful parts and the combined total matters as much as the split. Keep it to 2–3 series; label them in the legend.
charts.whenNotToUse: For a single series (use a plain bar chart), for part-of-whole at one moment (use a donut), or for continuous trends (use a line chart). Not for precise value read-off — pair with a table when exact numbers matter.
charts.interactions: Static presentation specimen — in-app the bars, tooltip, and legend toggle are data-bound (e.g. Recharts). Series colors map --chart-1 / --chart-2 to the Curie brand tokens (purple + teal); bar corners use --radius-xs and the card uses --radius-2xl.
charts.snippet:
Bar Chart — Stacked + Legend

January – June 2024

JanFebMarAprMayJun
DesktopMobile
Trending up by 5.2% this month
Showing total visitors for the last 6 months

charts.name: Donut Chart
charts.purpose: A donut (ring) chart that shows a categorical split of a whole, with the running total called out in the center. Rebuilt on-brand from the shadcn/recharts “donut with text” pattern as pure inline SVG — no chart library.
charts.whenToUse: For part-of-whole at a single moment across a handful of categories (≤ 6), when the total itself is worth showing (put it in the hole). Always label the segments — here via the legend.
charts.whenNotToUse: For precise comparisons between similar slices (use a bar chart), for many categories, or for change over time (use a line chart). Avoid full pies when the center total adds meaning — prefer the donut.
charts.interactions: Static presentation specimen — in-app the slices + tooltip are data-bound (e.g. Recharts). The 5-way categorical palette maps --chart-1…--chart-5 to Curie brand tokens (purple, teal, blue, light-purple, cyan); the ring hole (inner radius 60) holds the total in Manrope.
charts.snippet:
Pie Chart — Donut with Text

January – June 2024

ChromeSafariFirefoxEdgeOther
Trending up by 5.2% this month
Showing total visitors for the last 6 months

charts.name: Radial Gauge (Stacked)
charts.purpose: A semicircle radial gauge that stacks two parts of a total along a 180° arc, with the total called out in the center. Rebuilt on-brand from the shadcn/recharts “radial stacked” pattern as pure inline SVG (no chart library), framed for Curie: total briefs read, split by device.
charts.whenToUse: For a headline two-part total where the sum is the story and you want a compact, gauge-like readout (e.g. total briefs read = desktop + mobile). Label the parts in the legend.
charts.whenNotToUse: For more than 2–3 parts (use a donut or bar), for precise comparison (use a bar), or for trends over time (use a line chart).
charts.interactions: Static presentation specimen — in-app the arcs + tooltip are data-bound (e.g. Recharts). The two parts map --chart-1 / --chart-2 to Curie brand tokens (purple desktop + teal mobile); the arc is a 180° sweep with rounded ends and a faint full-arc track, total set in Manrope in the hole.
charts.snippet:
Radial Chart — Stacked

Briefs read · last 6 months

DesktopMobile
Trending up by 5.2% this month
Briefs read across desktop and mobile

charts.name: Radial (Text)
charts.purpose: A single-metric radial gauge: one figure on a 250° ring (gap at the bottom) called out big in the center. Rebuilt on-brand from the shadcn/recharts “radial with text” pattern as pure inline SVG, framed for Curie as CME credits earned.
charts.whenToUse: For one headline KPI that deserves a compact, glanceable ring (a count, a score, a streak). The arc is the accent; the number in the hole is the point.
charts.whenNotToUse: For multiple parts (use the stacked radial or donut) or for comparisons/trends (use bar/line). Don't overload the center with more than a number + a short label.
charts.interactions: Static presentation specimen — in-app the arc is data-bound (e.g. Recharts). The value arc uses the brand accent (teal, --chart-2) over a faint full-track; rounded ends, 250° sweep, big number in Manrope in the hole.
charts.snippet:
Radial Chart — Text

CME credits · earned this year

Trending up by 5.2% this month
Across your completed modules

charts.name: Line Chart
charts.purpose: A small multi-series (2) line chart as inline SVG with recessive gridlines, muted axis labels, thin 2px strokes, end markers, and a dot legend. Series colors follow the entity (blue / green), never rank, so identity is never carried by position alone.
charts.whenToUse: Use to show two related metrics changing over time — briefs read vs. summaries saved by month. Keep to two series; the legend names each so color is never the only cue.
charts.whenNotToUse: Do not use for a single metric where a stat-spark suffices, for comparing unrelated measures on different scales (never a dual y-axis — use two charts), or for more than a few series where lines tangle.
charts.interactions: Static presentation specimen — the monthly series are illustrative and would be bound in-app. No hover or JS; the legend + two token hues keep both series identifiable.
charts.snippet: Briefs readSummaries saved

charts.name: Sparkline
charts.purpose: A tiny inline trend line (inline SVG) that shows the shape of a short series at a glance, sized to sit beside a headline stat. The .stat-spark row pairs a big number, a delta, and the sparkline in one card.
charts.whenToUse: Use to add momentum context to a single metric — a KPI tile, a stat card, or inline beside a value in a table. Use .sparkline--up / .sparkline--down to tint the trend positive or negative.
charts.whenNotToUse: Do not use when exact values matter (use a bar-chart or line-chart with axes), for more than one series, or as a standalone chart — it has no axes or labels by design.
charts.interactions: Static presentation specimen — the 12-point series is illustrative and would be bound to live data in-app. No hover or JS; the tone modifier (--up / --down) is the only variant.
charts.snippet: Weekly brief engagement1,28418% vs. last week
